competitive_position: SAP (likely SAP Ariba) is the primary identified competitor and also the incumbent ERP, creating integration and ecosystem lock-in risk. SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat due to incumbent ERP advantage and likely government account presence, though no buyer calls have validated this or revealed other shortlisted vendors. Zycus differentiation hinges on AI-native unified S2P architecture (Agentic AI, single data core, faster deployment, lower TCO) versus SAP's retrofitted AI and fragmented suite. No competitive intelligence from SARS means threat level is inferred from market position and incumbent advantage, not buyer-stated preference. Recency: competitor identified in CRM 24 Mar 2026; no updates since.

champion.source: CRM MEDDPICC Champion field, 2026-03-24; zero buyer calls; no contact roles
champion.summary: Champion strength is weak. The CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) identifies the CPO (ex-colleague relationship, internal advocate) and the CTO (existing engagement, technical sponsor) as strong potential champions, but neither is named, no contact roles are mapped, and zero buyer calls confirm their advocacy, access to the tender evaluation committee, or current engagement in this RFP cycle. A historical relationship is not a current champion without active participation. The move is to activate the CPO/CTO relationship immediately and secure their guidance on next steps and shortlist intelligence.
champion.champion: CPO (unnamed, ex-colleague) and CTO (unnamed, existing engagement) per CRM MEDDPICC

items.area: Zero buyer calls and no mapped stakeholders beyond CRM-entered roles
items.quote: No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity or account; no contact roles on the opportunity; CPO and CTO identified in CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar) but unnamed and unengaged.
items.source: Avoma discovery (opp + account + attendee pulls returned zero buyer calls); Salesforce contact roles query

items.area: No competitive intelligence from buyer on who else is shortlisted or how SARS evaluates vendors
items.quote: SAP Ariba assumed primary competitor per CRM and living memory, but zero buyer calls mean no validation of shortlist composition, competitor positioning, or SARS scoring versus rivals.
items.source: Zero buyer calls; CRM Competition field 24 Mar 2026

items.area: POPIA compliance, data residency, and government cloud certification capability unconfirmed
items.quote: SARS RFP likely requires South African data residency and government compliance (POPIA, audit trails for revenue agency procurement). Zycus capability to meet these requirements is not documented in Salesforce or validated on a call.
items.source: CRM MEDDPICC Decision Criteria 24 Mar 2026; living memory risks; zero calls to validate capability

items.quote: Public-sector RFPs in South Africa may require or prefer a local systems-integration partner for implementation and support. Whether SARS RFP mandates this and whether Zycus has engaged a South African partner (beyond Letsema supporting role) is unknown.
items.source: Living memory risks; zero calls; RFP document not provided

recommended_moves: Activate the ex-colleague CPO relationship to secure shortlist intelligence and next-stage guidance.
Obtain the RFP 33/2025 document, scoring criteria, and post-shortlist evaluation timeline from SARS procurement.
Book a technical deep-dive with the CTO to validate Merlin Agentic AI differentiation versus SAP and other finalists.
next_step: JUne 24: Shortlisted and we will get informed soon

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) identifies the Economic Buyer as likely the CPO (described as ex-colleague relationship, owns procurement transformation) and the CTO (existing engagement, owns platform and architecture decision), with secondary influence from the CFO (cost scrutiny, pricing sensitivity). However, none of these individuals are named, no contact roles exist on the opportunity, and zero buyer calls have confirmed their authority, engagement, or sponsorship. The EB is structurally identified by role but not validated by name, active participation, or commercial conversation. This is a hygiene gap (the knowledge exists in CRM role form) but also a partial knowledge gap (no confirmation of who holds these roles at SARS, no evidence of their engagement, and no visibility into whether they are active in the RFP evaluation). The move is to map and engage the named CPO and CTO.
